Dockistrate End-to-End Flow and Function Reference

This document explains how Dockistrate runs in both interactive and CLI modes and documents every Bash function in the repo. It is intentionally verbose and focuses on what happens under the hood: inputs, validation, file/state changes, Docker actions, and call paths. Completion scripts and test harness/test case functions are included later in a compact format to keep the reference exhaustive.

Key conventions used in this file:

  • "Entry points" explains how a function is reached. Unless noted, CLI and interactive both call the same function via run_command.
  • "Reads/Writes" lists the main state/config files or Docker resources the function touches.
  • "Side effects" calls out container changes, file mutations, and backups.
  • "Interactive notes" only appear when the function behaves differently under INTERACTIVE=true.
  • Messages prefixed with [Error] are emitted to stderr so stdout remains parseable for automation.

End-to-End Flow (Interactive vs CLI)

Entry Script: dockistrate.sh

  • Loads version, lib/config.sh, and then sources modules via lib/loader.sh.
  • Eager-loads core modules needed for dispatch (logging, utils, dependencies, cli).
  • Initializes global flags: VERBOSE=false, INTERACTIVE=false.
  • Performs a lightweight pre-scan of argv to allow version, fix-permissions, and pre-runtime update helpers (help, help update, help-update, upgrade-preflight) to avoid normal runtime prep.
  • Parses -v/--verbose and -i/--interactive flags.
  • If verbose and the selected command is not pre-runtime: enables shell tracing and log file capture (via [ensure_log_writable](#fn-ensure_log_writable)).
  • Defers full runtime prep until the first real command path; prep sources deferred feature modules, then runs [check_dependencies](#fn-check_dependencies), [load_config](#fn-load_config), and [cleanup_leftovers](#fn-cleanup_leftovers) once per process. Pre-runtime update helpers skip this path.
  • If interactive: enters the task-oriented picker loop via [interactive_picker](#fn-interactive_picker), prepares runtime when a runtime command is selected, collects args via [prompt_args_for_command](#fn-prompt_args_for_command), reviews mutating commands via [review_command_before_run](#fn-review_command), dispatches via [run_command](#fn-run_command), and records successful selections through [interactive_record_recent_command](#fn-interactive_recents_helpers).
  • If CLI: prepares runtime immediately before dispatch unless the command is pre-runtime, then calls [run_command](#fn-run_command).

Interactive Mode Flow

  1. dockistrate.sh sets INTERACTIVE=true.
  2. [interactive_picker](#fn-interactive_picker) shows the home screen, task submenus, global search, or the advanced command browser.
  3. When a command is chosen, deferred runtime prep runs once (module load + dependency checks + config load) unless the command is a pre-runtime update helper.
  4. [prompt_args_for_command](#fn-prompt_args_for_command) (or specialized collectors) gathers args, validates input, and sends mutating commands through [review_command_before_run](#fn-review_command).
  5. When the review is accepted, SELECTED_CMD/SELECTED_ARGS are set; choosing Edit re-enters argument collection for the same command, and Cancel returns to the picker.
  6. [run_command](#fn-run_command) is called with the selected command and arguments.
  7. Successful interactive selections are stored in state/config/interactive_recent.csv unless a collected argument is marked sensitive by arg metadata; recents and favorites rerun through the same review path.
  8. The same core functions are executed as in CLI mode. Some functions check INTERACTIVE for prompts or defaults.

CLI Mode Flow

  1. dockistrate.sh parses flags and sets INTERACTIVE=false.
  2. Deferred runtime prep runs once (module load + dependency checks + config load), except for pre-runtime update helpers.
  3. [run_command](#fn-run_command) dispatches based on the command name.
  4. Commands validate input and run the same core functions as interactive mode.

Execution Flags and Behaviors

  • INTERACTIVE: enables prompts and menu selection logic. Some functions treat empty values as "keep current" in interactive mode.
  • VERBOSE: enables shell tracing, logs command traces, and prints verbose messages. Pre-runtime update helpers skip verbose log-file setup to remain no-write.

Internal test/orchestration controls exist in the codebase (for example, skip-Docker or skip-update switches), but they are not part of the supported operator configuration surface.

State and Configuration Layout

Environment variables:

Dockistrate does not expose operator-facing environment-variable overrides for

runtime roots or proxy identity. Internal test/orchestration controls mentioned

elsewhere in this file are not part of the supported operator configuration

surface.

Main state directory: state/.

Runtime state paths are guarded before creation, writes, and permission

normalization. Symlinked state roots or subdirectories such as state/config,

state/logs, state/certs, state/acme-webroot, and generated Nginx config

directories fail closed instead of being followed.

Key files (paths are resolved in lib/config/common.sh):

  • Fixed proxy container identity: nginx-proxy (commands refuse to manage a conflicting third-party container or another Dockistrate checkout’s proxy with that name).
  • state/config/state_schema_version: runtime state schema marker. Missing pre-schema state is treated as schema 1, and newer unsupported schema values fail bootstrap.
  • state/config/global_settings.csv: header setting_key,setting_value (HTTP version, TLS defaults, strict-mode ownership toggle, image settings, persisted NGINX_DOCKER_OPTS, etc).
  • state/config/backend_ports.csv: mixed record types with header record_type,domain,backend_upstream,network,path_prefix,header_set,listen_port,upstream_port,protocol,certificate_ref,websocket,redirect_enabled,redirect_code,http3_enabled,alt_svc,path_match,path_priority,path_target,path_rewrite,reason,source_location.
    • Backend rows (record_type=backend) use domain,backend_upstream,network.
    • Port rows (record_type=port) use domain,listen_port,upstream_port,protocol,certificate_ref,websocket,redirect_enabled,redirect_code,http3_enabled,alt_svc.
    • Path rows (record_type=path) use domain,path_prefix,header_set,listen_port,websocket,redirect_enabled,redirect_code,path_match,path_priority,path_target,path_rewrite,reason,source_location.
  • state/config/nginx_directives.csv: header scope,domain,listen_port,path_prefix,directive_mode,directive_name,directive_value (scope: global|backend|port|path|stream-global|stream-backend|stream-port, directive_mode: managed|raw).
  • state/config/backend_aliases.csv: header record_type,hostname,target_domain (record_type is alias or dedicated).
  • state/config/dedicated_host_inheritance.csv: header hostname,inherit_mtls,inherit_acl,inherit_security_rules,inherit_headers,inherit_paths.
  • state/config/custom_headers.csv: header header_type,header_name,header_value.
  • state/config/backend_headers.csv: header domain,header_type,header_name,header_value.
  • state/config/backend_http_versions.csv: header domain,http_version.
  • state/config/port_tls_protocols.csv: header listen_port,tls_protocols.
  • state/config/port_tls_ciphers.csv: header listen_port,tls_ciphers.
  • state/config/capture_tls_decrypt.state: TLS key capture session state (enabled, keylog_file, timestamp).
  • state/config/backend_mtls.csv: header domain,mtls_directory.
  • state/config/backend_client_ip_headers.csv: header domain,client_ip_header_name.
  • state/config/backend_proxy_ip_headers.csv: header domain,proxy_ip_header_name.
  • state/config/backend_docker_opts.csv: header key,docker_options.
  • state/config/backend_acl_policies.csv: header domain,acl_policy.
  • state/config/backend_acl_statuses.csv: header domain,acl_status_code.
  • state/config/backend_security_rule_statuses.csv: header domain,security_rule_status_code.
  • state/config/security_rules.csv: CSV rule DB with header enabled,domain,mode,status_code,condition_count,selector_1,name_1,condition_1,value_1,selector_2,name_2,condition_2,value_2,selector_3,name_3,condition_3,value_3,selector_4,name_4,condition_4,value_4,selector_5,name_5,condition_5,value_5,selector_6,name_6,condition_6,value_6,selector_7,name_7,condition_7,value_7,selector_8,name_8,condition_8,value_8,selector_9,name_9,condition_9,value_9,selector_10,name_10,condition_10,value_10,reason,source_location.
  • state/config/security_ip_rules.csv: CSV rule DB with header enabled,domain,scope,action,ip_value,status_code.
  • state/config/access_log_fields.csv: header log_field (one Nginx log variable per row for log_format).
  • state/config/interactive_recent.csv: interactive-only recent successful command selections, header timestamp,command,arg_count,args_csv, capped at 10 entries.
  • state/config/interactive_favorites.csv: interactive-only saved command selections, header command,arg_count,args_csv.

Generated Nginx config:

  • state/config/nginx_conf/nginx.conf
  • state/config/nginx_conf/conf.d/*.conf
  • state/config/nginx_conf/conf.d/security_ip/*.inc
  • state/config/nginx_conf/stream_conf/*.conf
  • state/config/nginx_conf/stream_conf/security_ip/*.inc

Runtime directories:

  • state/certs/ for certificate storage (letsencrypt, selfsigned, custom, mtls).
  • state/logs/ for logs.
  • state/backups/ for backups and transaction archives.
  • state/pcaps/ for packet captures.
  • state/tmp/ for temp files.

Default runtime permission policy:

  • Directories: state, config, logs, logs/errors, acme-webroot, certs -> 750.
  • Directories: tmp, pcaps, backups -> 700.
  • Files: global_settings.csv, state_schema_version, docker_manager.log, audit.log -> 640.
  • Files: backend_docker_opts.csv, capture_tls_decrypt.state, *.tar.gz, *.pcap -> 600.

Function Reference by Module

Note: Unless explicitly stated, interactive and CLI flows converge at run_command and execute the same function bodies. Interactive differences are only called out when the function reads INTERACTIVE or adds prompts.

Module: loader (lib/loader/*.sh)

_source_modules (lib/loader/_source_modules.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate.sh when sourcing core modules.
  • Inputs: list of file paths.
  • Behavior: sequentially source each given file.
  • Side effects: loads functions/variables into the current shell.

Module: config (lib/config/*.sh)

load_config (lib/config/load_config.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate_prepare_runtime in dockistrate.sh (first command path).
  • Inputs: none; relies on CONFIG_DIR, STATE_DIR, etc.
  • Behavior: rejects symlinked runtime state paths, creates state directories, ensures log fields file, loads or writes global_settings.csv, normalizes image tags, persists updates if needed, then enforces runtime state permissions.
  • Reads/Writes: GLOBAL_SETTINGS_FILE, ACCESS_LOG_FIELDS_FILE, state directories.
  • Side effects: may update config defaults, save them, and chmod runtime state paths/files.

state_schema_bootstrap (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: bootstrap_config_runtime.
  • Behavior: reads the state schema marker, treats missing pre-schema state as schema 1, rejects invalid or newer unsupported schema values, runs the idempotent migration dispatcher, and writes the current marker atomically only when the marker is missing or a migration advances it.
  • Reads/Writes: state/config/state_schema_version.
  • Side effects: creates the schema marker when missing or updates it after a migration; existing current markers are not rewritten.

state_schema_version_file (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: state schema bootstrap helpers and tests.
  • Behavior: returns the schema marker path under the current CONFIG_DIR.

_state_schema_normalize_version (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: state schema marker read, write, and migration helpers.
  • Behavior: validates schema versions as positive bounded numeric values, strips leading zeroes, and rejects values too large for portable shell arithmetic.

_state_schema_read_marker (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: state_schema_bootstrap.
  • Behavior: validates the schema marker as a single positive numeric value, preserves final lines without trailing newlines, rejects multiline marker content, and rejects newer versions than this release supports.

_state_schema_read_marker_for_supported (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: upgrade_preflight and _state_schema_read_marker.
  • Behavior: read-only marker check against a supplied supported schema version. Missing marker is schema 1; malformed, multiline, overlarge, or newer unsupported markers fail without writing state.

state_schema_read_marker_readonly (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: upgrade_preflight.
  • Behavior: public read-only wrapper for checking the current on-disk schema marker without creating, repairing, or migrating state.

_state_schema_write_marker (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: state_schema_bootstrap.
  • Behavior: writes the schema marker atomically with restricted file mode.

_state_schema_migrate (lib/config/state_schema.sh)

  • Entry points: state_schema_bootstrap.
  • Behavior: provides the future migration dispatch point. Schema 1 is currently the public baseline and has no migration steps.

save_config (lib/config/save_config.sh)

  • Entry points: called by global settings setters.
  • Inputs: in-memory globals like HTTP_VERSION, NGINX_IMAGE, etc.
  • Behavior: writes GLOBAL_SETTINGS_FILE with current values.
  • Side effects: overwrites global settings file.

normalize_image_with_latest (lib/config/normalize_image_with_latest.sh)

  • Entry points: load_config, set_certbot_image.
  • Inputs: image reference.
  • Behavior: appends :latest when no tag/digest is provided.
  • Returns: normalized image string.

image_uses_latest_tag (lib/config/image_uses_latest_tag.sh)

  • Entry points: pull_image_if_autopull, describe_image_with_local_state.
  • Inputs: image reference.
  • Behavior: detects if the image uses the :latest tag (not a digest).

pull_image_if_autopull (lib/config/pull_image_if_autopull.sh)

  • Entry points: recreate_nginx_container, certbot flows.
  • Inputs: image, label.
  • Behavior: skips pulls when SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true; honors pull mode (always|if-missing|never) for NGINX_IMAGE/CERTBOT_IMAGE; skips pull for pinned tags; checks local image presence for if-missing.
  • Side effects: may docker pull the image, emits info/warn messages.

Module: logging (lib/logging/*.sh)

log_msg (lib/logging/log_msg.sh)

  • Entry points: many command functions.
  • Inputs: message string.
  • Behavior: writes timestamped logs to LOG_FILE; honors ENABLE_LOGGING and VERBOSE.
  • Side effects: creates/updates log files, may echo to stdout/stderr.

audit_log (lib/logging/audit_log.sh)

  • Entry points: run_command (all commands).
  • Inputs: command string.
  • Behavior: normalizes control characters to single-line text, rotates audit log if >1MB, appends timestamped entry.
  • Side effects: writes to AUDIT_LOG_FILE.

ensure_log_writable (lib/logging/ensure_log_writable.sh)

  • Entry points: log_msg, audit_log, verbose setup.
  • Inputs: file path.
  • Behavior: rejects symlinked runtime log paths, ensures log directory exists, touches file, fixes ownership if running as root, sets permissions.
  • Side effects: modifies permissions and ownership.

_effective_user (lib/logging/_effective_user.sh)

  • Entry points: ensure_log_writable.
  • Behavior: returns invoking user (prefers SUDO_USER when root).

ensure_root (lib/logging/ensure_root.sh)

  • Entry points: dependency installation, Docker service start.
  • Behavior: exits unless running as root.

Module: dependencies (lib/dependencies/*.sh)

check_dependencies (lib/dependencies/check_dependencies.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate_prepare_runtime in dockistrate.sh (first command path).
  • Behavior: runs Docker and OpenSSL checks unless SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true; ensures backup dir exists.

check_docker_installed (lib/dependencies/check_docker_installed.sh)

  • Entry points: check_dependencies.
  • Behavior: verifies Docker binary; optionally installs in interactive mode.
  • Interactive notes: prompts to install Docker.

ensure_docker_running (lib/dependencies/ensure_docker_running.sh)

  • Entry points: check_dependencies.
  • Behavior: ensures Docker daemon is running; starts Docker Desktop on macOS or systemd service on Linux.
  • Interactive notes: prompts user if systemctl is unavailable.

check_docker_access (lib/dependencies/check_docker_access.sh)

  • Entry points: check_dependencies.
  • Behavior: confirms current user can run Docker commands.

check_openssl_installed (lib/dependencies/check_openssl_installed.sh)

  • Entry points: check_dependencies.
  • Behavior: verifies openssl is available; can install in interactive mode.

install_package (lib/dependencies/install_package.sh)

  • Entry points: dependency installers.
  • Behavior: installs packages via Homebrew (macOS) or apt/yum (Linux).

Module: utils (lib/utils/*.sh)

_realpath_portable (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: normalize_cert_dir, relativize_cert_dir.
  • Behavior: resolves absolute path without relying on GNU-only tools; follows symlinks safely.

normalize_domain (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: most domain functions.
  • Behavior: lowercases domains for consistent matching.

sanitize_domain_name (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: container naming and file naming.
  • Behavior: replaces non-alphanumerics with -.

is_valid_domain (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: input validation.
  • Behavior: regex validation for domain format.

is_valid_port (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: port input validation.
  • Behavior: numeric 1..65535 validation.

is_valid_optional_port (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: certificate port suffix prompts.
  • Behavior: allows empty or valid port.

is_valid_protocol (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: backend/port commands.
  • Behavior: accepts http|https|tcp.

is_valid_http_version (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI validators.
  • Behavior: accepts http1.0|http1.1|http2.

is_valid_header_name (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: header setters.
  • Behavior: strict header token validation.

is_header_or_off (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI validators for header overrides.
  • Behavior: allows off or valid header name.

is_status_code (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: ACL/security status setters.
  • Behavior: validates HTTP status 100-599.

is_yes_no (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: ws and expose flags.
  • Behavior: accepts yes|no.

is_on_off (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: set-port-redirect, real IP recursive.
  • Behavior: accepts on|off.

is_true_false (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: auto backup and compression flags.
  • Behavior: accepts true|false.

is_valid_ipv4 (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: IP validation helpers.
  • Behavior: checks IPv4 format and octet ranges.

is_valid_cidr (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: trusted proxies, ACL.
  • Behavior: IPv4 CIDR validation.

is_valid_ip_or_cidr (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: ACL/trusted proxy validation.
  • Behavior: accepts IPv4 or CIDR.

is_valid_image_ref (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: image setters.
  • Behavior: simplified Docker image reference validation.

is_valid_network_name (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx render-time state validation.
  • Behavior: validates Docker network names in backend state rows.

is_valid_redirect_code_spec (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx render-time state validation.
  • Behavior: validates redirect status codes (301|302|308) with optional target port suffix (:port).

file_exists (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: certificate upload prompts.
  • Behavior: returns success if file exists.

normalize_cert_dir (lib/utils/certs.sh)

  • Entry points: certificate paths in add_cert, add_port, update_port, etc.
  • Behavior: normalizes certificate path under CERTS_DIR; allows external absolute paths only when explicitly enabled by callers; keeps relative traversal outside CERTS_DIR blocked.

relativize_cert_dir (lib/utils/certs.sh)

  • Entry points: certificate path storage.
  • Behavior: converts absolute certificate paths back to CERTS_DIR-relative form for storage.

is_existing_cert_dir (lib/utils/certs.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI validator.
  • Behavior: validates that a certificate directory exists under CERTS_DIR.

backend_aliases_file (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: alias CRUD.
  • Behavior: resolves alias file path; sets BACKEND_ALIASES_FILE if not set.

backend_exists (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: most backend operations.
  • Behavior: checks for backend record in BACKEND_PORTS_FILE.

alias_exists (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: alias CRUD.
  • Behavior: checks backend_aliases.csv for an alias record.

backend_for_alias (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: resolve alias to backend.
  • Behavior: returns target domain if alias exists and backend is present.

primary_domain_for (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: domain resolution.
  • Behavior: resolves alias to primary backend or returns normalized input.

domain_exists (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: header/ACL/backends.
  • Behavior: returns true if domain is backend or alias.

list_domain_aliases (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: Nginx config, listing.
  • Behavior: lists aliases mapped to a domain.

container_exists (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: container ops.
  • Behavior: checks docker for container existence.

remove_container_and_anonymous_volumes (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: container-removal flows.
  • Behavior: removes a container via docker rm -f -v, cleaning up attached anonymous volumes.

container_status (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: status reports.
  • Behavior: returns Docker container state string.

container_running (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: start/capture/status.
  • Behavior: wraps container_status equality to running.

container_published_ports (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx update logic.
  • Behavior: returns sorted host ports published by a container.

is_host_port_listening (lib/utils/ports_runtime.sh)

  • Entry points: listen-port preflight checks.
  • Behavior: detects whether a host TCP port is already listening (via lsof, with ss/netstat fallbacks).

port_listener_owner_info (lib/utils/ports_runtime.sh)

  • Entry points: listen-port conflict messaging.
  • Behavior: resolves pid|process ownership for a listening host port when discoverable.

is_port_mapped_in_state (lib/utils/ports_runtime.sh)

  • Entry points: listen-port preflight checks.
  • Behavior: checks whether a port is already present in Dockistrate port records, with optional skip context.

suggest_free_port (lib/utils/ports_runtime.sh)

  • Entry points: listen-port conflict messaging.
  • Behavior: suggests a free alternative port by scanning nearby candidates and fallback ports while avoiding active listeners and already-mapped ports.

assert_host_port_available_or_fail (lib/utils/ports_runtime.sh)

  • Entry points: backend/port/nginx preflight checks.
  • Behavior: fails fast when a requested host port is occupied, prints PID/process details when available, and prints a concrete free-port recommendation when available.

ensure_network_exists (lib/utils/docker.sh)

  • Entry points: backend and nginx network operations.
  • Behavior: creates Docker network if missing.

escape_sed_literal (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: removal operations.
  • Behavior: escapes regex chars for safe sed patterns.

sed_in_place (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: most file edits.
  • Behavior: cross-platform sed -i for GNU/BSD.

move_line (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: reordering ACL/security/log fields.
  • Behavior: moves a line within a file using awk.

replace_line (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: security IP updates.
  • Behavior: replaces a line by line number.

nginx_header_var (lib/utils/state_helpers.sh)

  • Entry points: header variable mapping.
  • Behavior: converts a header name to $http_<name> variable.

ensure_valid_or_prompt (lib/utils/prompts.sh)

  • Entry points: many command functions.
  • Behavior: validates input; in interactive mode, prompts until valid.

normalize_validated_value (lib/utils/validators.sh)

  • Entry points: ensure_valid_or_prompt.
  • Behavior: normalizes validated values (e.g., lowercasing domains).

prompt_input (lib/utils/prompts.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive flows.
  • Behavior: reads a value with a default only when INTERACTIVE=true.

prompt_input_valid (lib/utils/prompts.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive flows.
  • Behavior: repeatedly prompts until validation passes.

read_with_editing (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: reads input with a default if empty; safe for non-tty.

confirm_prompt (lib/utils/prompts.sh)

  • Entry points: destructive operations.
  • Behavior: shared confirmation helper with 2 modes. yes_no prompts when interactive and otherwise auto-confirms; strict_yes requires interactive mode and exact YES, and fails in non-interactive use.

cleanup_leftovers (lib/utils/fs.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate_prepare_runtime in dockistrate.sh.
  • Behavior: deletes stale nginx include files that can break config.
  • Side effects: removes specific files in NGINX_HTTP_CONF_DIR.

Module: CLI and Interactive (lib/cli/*.sh)

run_command (lib/cli/run_command.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate.sh (CLI or interactive dispatch).
  • Inputs: command name and arguments.
  • Behavior: logs the command via audit_log unless dockistrate_command_skips_runtime_prep marks it as pre-runtime, prints a verbose trace when VERBOSE=true, then dispatches to the appropriate function.
  • Side effects: emits verbose output to stderr and triggers command execution.

dockistrate_command_skips_runtime_prep (lib/cli/pre_runtime_commands.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate.sh, interactive_picker_run_command_prompt, run_command.
  • Behavior: central predicate for commands that must run before runtime prep and without audit/config bootstrap side effects: help, help update, help-update, and upgrade-preflight.

interactive_picker (lib/cli/interactive_picker.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive mode loop in dockistrate.sh.
  • Behavior: shows a task-oriented home screen for common flows, including adding a backend, recent commands, favorites, port updates, service lifecycle, certificates, security rules, update helpers, diagnostics, global command search, and the advanced command browser. The home prompt includes interactive_dashboard_summary when available. When a command is chosen, it ensures deferred runtime prep has run unless the command is pre-runtime, checks interactive-only state availability with interactive_command_availability, gathers args via prompt_args_for_command, and re-enters that prompt when review-before-run asks to edit previous answers.
  • Interactive notes: recent/favorite reruns reuse review_command_before_run before setting SELECTED_CMD/SELECTED_ARGS; read-only commands bypass review, while mutating and destructive commands keep the same review and confirmation behavior. Global search matches command names, aliases, descriptions, and category names across all interactive categories. The advanced command browser preserves the category menu with focused backend override and global setting groups, while update helpers are also available from the home screen; when launched from home it can return to the home screen without exiting interactive mode. Category-local filtering and special flows for ACL/security rules are preserved. If a selected command requires missing state, command lists annotate the row before selection and the picker still shows guidance with a suggested setup action, return, or quit after selection. Supported menus expose contextual ? help without changing the current selection or filter.

interactive_command_browser (lib/cli/interactive_picker.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive_picker home screen.
  • Behavior: shows the advanced category browser, including the global search entry and all interactive command categories; in direct mode, Quit preserves the previous top-level picker exit behavior, while home-launched mode exposes Back to home and a distinct Quit path.

prompt_args_for_command (lib/cli/prompt_args_for_command.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive picker.
  • Behavior: resets per-prompt dynamic-choice caches, resolves argument specs, renders friendly labels/help/examples through arg_metadata, prompts for values, validates, routes mutating commands through review_command_before_run, and prepares accepted args for run_command.
  • Interactive notes: supports multi-line manual entry for docker_opts, maps Keep current sentinels to the current backend value, supports an explicit clear sentinel to remove stored backend docker opts, lets generic multi-argument prompts backtrack to the previous prompted argument, and lets review-before-run return to editing after all arguments are collected. The raw arg spec remains the machine-readable source; labels and examples are display metadata only.
  • Interactive notes: delegates to header and security handlers for set-hsts, set-csp, set-backend-hsts, set-backend-csp, and add-acl, plus post-processing for path and image commands.

collect_add_backend_interactive (lib/cli/collect_add_backend_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command for add-backend.
  • Behavior: step-by-step wizard for domain, protocol, network, image, ports, certificates, redirect, websocket, exposure, docker opts; HTTPS certificate choices include existing cert directories plus self-signed and Let's Encrypt generation; validates listen-port availability and re-prompts on host port conflicts.
  • Interactive notes: docker opts input accepts multi-line entry and is normalized before persistence.
  • Side effects: none directly; produces SELECTED_ARGS for add_backend.

collect_add_host_alias_interactive (lib/cli/collect_add_host_alias_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command for add-host-alias.
  • Behavior: forces backend selection (HTTP/HTTPS only) and validates alias.

get_arg_spec (lib/cli/get_arg_spec.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: returns semicolon-separated name|default specs for each command.

get_arg_choices (lib/cli/get_arg_choices.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: dispatches to __arg_choices_<arg> helpers to produce dynamic menu choices based on current args and state files.
  • Interactive notes: uses Docker to list images/networks when available.

arg_choices_images helpers (lib/cli/arg_choices_images.sh)

  • Entry points: get_arg_choices.
  • Behavior: provides __arg_choices_image, __arg_choices_container_port, __arg_choices_nginx_image, __arg_choices_certbot_image.

arg_choices_network_docker helpers (lib/cli/arg_choices_network_docker.sh)

  • Entry points: get_arg_choices.
  • Behavior: provides __arg_choices_network, __arg_choices_docker_opts.

arg_choices_protocols helpers (lib/cli/arg_choices_protocols.sh)

  • Entry points: get_arg_choices.
  • Behavior: provides __arg_choices_protocol, __arg_choices_ws, __arg_choices_listen, __arg_choices_version.

arg_choices_security helpers (lib/cli/arg_choices_security.sh)

  • Entry points: get_arg_choices.
  • Behavior: provides __arg_choices_mode, __arg_choices_allow_or_deny, __arg_choices_policy, __arg_choices_code, __arg_choices_source, __arg_choices_condition.

arg_choices_misc helpers (lib/cli/arg_choices_misc.sh)

  • Entry points: get_arg_choices.
  • Behavior: provides __arg_choices_domain, __arg_choices_header, __arg_choices_client_name, __arg_choices_nginx_port, __arg_choices_path_prefix, __arg_choices_port, __arg_choices_backup, __arg_choices_true_or_false, __arg_choices_on_off, __arg_choices_expose, __arg_choices_req_resp, __arg_choices_cert_path, __arg_choices_id.

prompt_args_handlers_headers (lib/cli/prompt_args_handlers_headers.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: handles header-related interactive flows for set-hsts, set-csp, set-backend-hsts, set-backend-csp.

prompt_args_handlers_security (lib/cli/prompt_args_handlers_security.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: dispatcher for security interactive flows; routes add-acl, add-security-rule, and update-security-rule to specialized handlers.

prompt_args_handle_add_acl_interactive (lib/cli/security_acl_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_handle_security_specials.
  • Behavior: interactive argument collection for add-acl, preserving existing defaults, Back semantics, and deny-only status-code prompt.

security_rule_interactive_common helpers (lib/cli/security_rule_interactive_common.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_handle_add_security_rule_interactive, prompt_args_handle_update_security_rule_interactive.
  • Behavior: provides _sr_prompt_* helpers and _sr_collect_condition_quads, including SR_COLLECTED_CONDS and SR_COLLECTED_SUMMARIES reset/collection behavior.

prompt_args_handle_add_security_rule_interactive (lib/cli/security_rule_add_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_handle_security_specials.
  • Behavior: interactive add-security-rule flow with condition collection, summary display, confirm/back flow, and unchanged arg assembly.

prompt_args_handle_update_security_rule_interactive (lib/cli/security_rule_update_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_handle_security_specials.
  • Behavior: interactive update-security-rule flow including current-rule parsing, replace-conditions branch, and no-change abort behavior.

prompt_args_handlers_defaults (lib/cli/prompt_args_handlers_defaults.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: computes per-command defaults (update-backend, headers, ACL/security status, TLS overrides, redirects, server tokens, log fields).

prompt_args_handlers_postprocess (lib/cli/prompt_args_handlers_postprocess.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: rewrites args for path options, start-nginx, set-nginx-docker-opts, image setters, and interactive upgrade-preflight optional flags.

_validator_for (lib/cli/_validator_for.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: maps argument names to validator functions.

arg_option_hint (lib/cli/arg_option_hint.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: supplies hint strings and examples for arguments.

arg_metadata (lib/cli/arg_metadata.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command.
  • Behavior: maps raw arg names to user-facing labels, help text, examples, empty-input notes, review labels, and sensitivity flags without changing CLI arg specs or command dispatch.

review_command helpers (lib/cli/review_command.sh)

  • Entry points: prompt_args_for_command and specialized interactive collectors.
  • Behavior: classifies commands as read-only, mutating, or destructive; formats the equivalent CLI invocation with shell-safe quoting; renders a human-readable argument summary using arg_metadata; and asks the operator to Run, Edit previous answers, or Cancel before mutating commands execute. The specialized add-backend review labels redirect answers and renders flag-based CLI equivalents plus a follow-up redirect command when needed.
  • Interactive notes: read-only commands such as status, status-all, check-config, tail-proxy-logs, list-*, and show-* bypass the review screen. Destructive commands require exact YES confirmation after choosing Run.

interactive_no_state helpers (lib/cli/interactive_no_state.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive_picker_run_command_prompt.
  • Behavior: detects interactive-only missing-state cases before argument collection, including no backends, no port mappings, no certificates, no backups, no backend headers, no mTLS-enabled backends, no ACL rules, and no security rules.
  • Interactive notes: renders a guidance screen through choose_option with a suggested setup command, return to the previous menu, or quit. Command pickers also reuse the availability result to append short display suffixes such as unavailable: no port mappings. restore-backup uses soft wording when no local backups are listed because the explicit backup-path prompt remains available. This does not change CLI dispatch or non-interactive command behavior.

interactive_dashboard helpers (lib/cli/interactive_dashboard.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive_picker.
  • Behavior: renders a best-effort home dashboard summary with proxy state, backend count, port count, certificate directory count, backup count, and capture state.
  • Interactive notes: state-file counts do not require Docker; proxy and capture state return unknown before deferred runtime prep, when Docker is unavailable, or when Docker cannot be queried. Helpers do not modify state.

interactive_recents helpers (lib/cli/interactive_recents.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate.sh interactive loop after successful run_command, interactive_picker home screen.
  • Reads/Writes: state/config/interactive_recent.csv, state/config/interactive_favorites.csv.
  • Behavior: stores successful interactive command selections as CSV-safe rows with timestamp, command, arg count, and nested CSV-encoded args; keeps only the latest 10 recents; skips recording commands whose collected args include metadata-marked sensitive arguments; loads and displays recent/favorite commands for rerun.
  • Interactive notes: recent and favorite reruns call review_command_before_run before selection. Read-only commands rerun without review; mutating commands show the review screen, and destructive commands keep exact-confirmation behavior. Recent entries can be added to or removed from favorites from the saved-command action menu.

build_condition_choices_for_selector (lib/cli/build_condition_choices_for_selector.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive security rule wizard.
  • Behavior: filters allowed operators based on selector type and formats examples.

choose_option (lib/cli/choose_option.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive menus.
  • Behavior: terminal menu with shared header/footer rendering, arrow keys, numeric selection, Enter selection, Q/Esc back handling, contextual ? help, and DOCKISTRATE_NO_CLEAR=true support via cli_clear_screen; returns selected index.

choose_command_with_filter (lib/cli/choose_command_with_filter.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive category command menus.
  • Behavior: filters the commands passed by the caller using raw command names, aliases, descriptions, and any interactive availability suffix; uses shared header/footer rendering; supports / or S search mode, C clear, contextual ? help, Q/Esc back, numeric selection, and returns the selected command.

choose_global_command_with_filter (lib/cli/choose_command_with_filter.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive global command search.
  • Behavior: flattens all interactive command categories, dedupes commands, displays category context, filters by command name, alias, description, category, or any interactive availability suffix, and returns the selected command.

interactive_picker_menu_data helpers (lib/cli/interactive_picker_menu_data.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive picker and global command search.
  • Behavior: maps category labels to command arrays and flattens all interactive categories while preserving first-category order for duplicate commands.

render_interactive helpers (lib/cli/render_interactive.sh)

  • Entry points: choose_option, choose_command_with_filter, and global command search.
  • Behavior: provides shared interactive screen helpers: cli_clear_screen, cli_read_escape_sequence, cli_render_header, cli_render_footer, cli_render_breadcrumb, cli_render_help_line, cli_interactive_help_for_context, and cli_show_interactive_help. cli_clear_screen skips terminal clearing when DOCKISTRATE_NO_CLEAR=true; contextual help is best-effort and returns to the same menu after Enter, Esc, or Q.

choose_http_domain (lib/cli/choose_http_domain.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: lists backends and lets user pick one.

choose_alias_backend (lib/cli/choose_alias_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: alias creation wizard.
  • Behavior: picks a backend with HTTP/HTTPS port mappings.

read_lines_into_array (lib/cli/read_lines_into_array.sh)

  • Entry points: menu helpers.
  • Behavior: fills array from newline-delimited strings; uses mapfile if available.

command_alias (lib/cli/command_alias.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive command display.
  • Behavior: converts command name to a human-friendly label.

_set_command_description (lib/cli/_set_command_description.sh)

  • Entry points: command description files.
  • Behavior: stores description into a Bash 3 compatible variable map.

command_description (lib/cli/command_description.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive picker.
  • Behavior: fetches description for a command.

format_command_display (lib/cli/format_command_display.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive picker.
  • Behavior: wraps command label + description to terminal width.

usage (lib/cli/usage.sh)

  • Entry points: dockistrate.sh when command missing or invalid.
  • Behavior: prints CLI usage and command list.

help_update (lib/cli/update_help.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI help-update and help update.
  • Behavior: prints the git-native local update workflow: create backup, fetch tags, run target preflight, checkout tag, run post-checkout preflight, then check-config if a managed proxy exists. Tells production users to run from release tags, not main, notes detached HEAD at a release tag is expected, and says git pull is not the production update workflow.

help_command (lib/cli/update_help.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI help.
  • Behavior: routes help update to help_update; unsupported topics print normal usage and return usage status.

upgrade_preflight (lib/cli/upgrade_preflight.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI and interactive upgrade-preflight.
  • Behavior: read-only compatibility check for current on-disk state and optional local release tag. It validates the current schema marker, reports backup presence, optionally enforces --require-backup, validates stable local tags via refs/tags/<tag>, reads target VERSION and schema metadata through git -C "$SCRIPT_DIR" show, requires the tag name to match the target VERSION, and fails downgrades with cannot downgrade state from N to M.
  • Reads/Writes: reads state/config/state_schema_version, direct children of state/backups/, bounded backup archive member listings, and local git tag metadata. Writes nothing.
  • Side effects: none; no Docker checks, runtime bootstrap, audit logging, migrations, config repair, remote fetch, or update application.

upgrade_preflight helpers (lib/cli/upgrade_preflight.sh)

  • Entry points: upgrade_preflight.
  • Behavior: includes upgrade_preflight_usage, upgrade_preflight_error_usage, upgrade_preflight_validate_tag, upgrade_preflight_validate_version, upgrade_preflight_git_has_local_tag, upgrade_preflight_git_show_file, upgrade_preflight_parse_schema_constant, upgrade_preflight_read_target_version, upgrade_preflight_read_target_schema, upgrade_preflight_check_tag_version_match, upgrade_preflight_backup_exists, upgrade_preflight_backup_archive_is_full, upgrade_preflight_backup_archive_validate_listing, upgrade_preflight_check_backup, upgrade_preflight_compare_schema, and upgrade_preflight_parse_args. Backup archive inspection streams tar -tzf output through bounded entry-count and entry-length validation while requiring all members to stay under the archive root and include config/. Historical tag shell is parsed as text only; legacy common.sh fallback accepts exactly CURRENT_STATE_SCHEMA_VERSION="N" with N > 0.

has_backends (lib/cli/has_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: argument prompt guards.
  • Behavior: returns true if any backend exists.

cmd_requires_existing_backend (lib/cli/cmd_requires_existing_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompt guard.
  • Behavior: lists commands that require existing backends.

is_back_input (lib/cli/is_back_input.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompt loops.
  • Behavior: detects "back" sentinel input.

no_domain_overrides_message (lib/cli/no_domain_overrides_message.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: prints context-sensitive "no overrides" message and pauses.

no_header_overrides_message (lib/cli/no_header_overrides_message.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: prints "no headers" message and pauses.

no_port_tls_overrides_message (lib/cli/no_port_tls_overrides_message.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive TLS overrides.
  • Behavior: prints message when no TLS overrides/ports exist.

mark_current_option (lib/cli/mark_current_option.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: annotates current selection in a choice list.

summarize_cond (lib/cli/summarize_cond.sh)

  • Entry points: security rule interactive updates.
  • Behavior: formats condition summary strings.

list_https_ports (lib/cli/list_https_ports.sh)

  • Entry points: TLS override prompts.
  • Behavior: returns HTTPS ports (443 plus mapped HTTPS ports).

Module: backends (lib/backends/*.sh)

add_backend (lib/backends/add_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-backend; interactive wizard -> run_command.
  • Inputs: domain, image, container_port, protocol, and optional --listen, --cert selfsigned|letsencrypt|none|path, --ws, --docker-opts, --network, --expose.
  • Behavior: validates inputs, normalizes domain, ensures no alias collision, determines listen port defaults, preflights host-port availability for newly introduced listeners, handles HTTPS certificate selection (none and selfsigned mint self-signed certs; letsencrypt reuses the Certbot add-cert flow), enforces certificate directory containment under state/certs, normalizes docker options to canonical single-line storage, ensures network, starts container (unless SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS, which uses a 127.0.0.1 placeholder IP), writes backend and port records, optionally adds HTTP redirect for HTTPS, and removes the newly created backend container during rollback if a downstream config/runtime step fails.
  • Reads/Writes: BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, BACKEND_DOCKER_OPTS_FILE, certificate directories.
  • Side effects: docker run, new config entries, backups, update_nginx_config.
  • Interactive notes: uses positional args from wizard and can prompt for certificate paths and redirect.

update_backend (lib/backends/update_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI update-backend; interactive prompts.
  • Inputs: domain plus --image, --container-port, --docker-opts, --network.
  • Behavior: resolves alias, loads current backend entry, normalizes docker options to canonical storage before use, optionally recreates container when image or docker opts change, updates network attachments (including hyphenated network names), updates stored IP/port, and updates port mappings if container port changes. Recreate flows stage the existing container under a rollback name, preserve anonymous volumes, and only delete the superseded container after update_nginx_config succeeds. Network-change flows track runtime mutations and restore container network attachments during rollback when a downstream failure occurs.
  • Reads/Writes: BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, BACKEND_DOCKER_OPTS_FILE.
  • Side effects: may docker rename, docker rm -f, docker run, docker network connect, backup transaction, update_nginx_config.
  • Interactive notes: blank docker opts means keep current; explicit clear selection removes stored docker opts; avoids restart unless changed.

replace_backend_network (lib/backends/replace_backend_network.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI replace-backend-network.
  • Behavior: wrapper over update_backend --network.

remove_backend (lib/backends/remove_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-backend.
  • Behavior: resolves alias, prompts before removing an existing backend container in interactive mode, and aborts the whole command if that confirmation is declined. When removal proceeds, Dockistrate first cleans related config entries, path headers, TLS overrides, ACL/security rule overrides, alias mappings, and mTLS material, then removes the backend container with attached anonymous volumes only after update_nginx_config succeeds. Destructive file/dir deletes are constrained to expected state roots via safe-delete guards.
  • Reads/Writes: multiple backend config files, BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, SECURITY_RULES_DB, SECURITY_IP_RULES_DB.
  • Side effects: removes Docker container and attached anonymous volumes after config update success, deletes include files, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_all_backends (lib/backends/remove_all_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-all-backends.
  • Behavior: iterates all backend records and calls remove_backend.
  • Side effects: removes backend containers/config via remove_backend, creates a backup when removals occur.

start_backend (lib/backends/start_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI start-backend.
  • Behavior: starts container for backend domain.
  • Side effects: docker start, backup entry.

stop_backend (lib/backends/stop_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI stop-backend.
  • Behavior: stops backend container.
  • Side effects: docker stop, backup entry.

restart_backend (lib/backends/restart_backend.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI restart-backend.
  • Behavior: restarts backend container.
  • Side effects: docker restart, backup entry.

start_all_backends (lib/backends/start_all_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI start-all-backends.
  • Behavior: starts all backend containers, updates nginx (refreshes backend IPs during the update).
  • Side effects: docker start for each container, update_nginx_config, backup entry.

stop_all_backends (lib/backends/stop_all_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI stop-all-backends.
  • Behavior: stops all backend containers.
  • Side effects: docker stop for each container, backup entry.

restart_all_backends (lib/backends/restart_all_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI restart-all-backends.
  • Behavior: restarts all backend containers, updates nginx (refreshes backend IPs during the update).
  • Side effects: docker restart for each container, update_nginx_config, backup entry.

list_backends (lib/backends/list_backends.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-backends, status, status-all.
  • Behavior: prints backend table with aliases, images, networks, overrides, and port summary.

list_host_aliases (lib/backends/list_host_aliases.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-host-aliases.
  • Behavior: prints alias -> backend mappings (optionally filtered by domain).

add_host_alias (lib/backends/add_host_alias.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-host-alias; interactive wizard.
  • Behavior: validates alias and backend, ensures backend has an HTTP/HTTPS port mapping, writes alias mapping.
  • Side effects: writes backend_aliases.csv, backups, nginx update.

remove_host_alias (lib/backends/remove_host_alias.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-host-alias.
  • Behavior: removes alias record by alias.
  • Side effects: updates backend_aliases.csv, backups, update_nginx_config.

backend_has_httpish_port (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: alias validation.
  • Behavior: checks whether domain has HTTP/HTTPS port mapping.

get_backend_port (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI prompts and updates.
  • Behavior: returns backend container port from BACKEND_PORTS_FILE.

get_backend_image (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI prompts and status.
  • Behavior: reads running container image for a backend.

get_backend_network (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI prompts and update.
  • Behavior: returns stored network or default.

get_backend_docker_opts (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive prompts.
  • Behavior: reads docker opts from BACKEND_DOCKER_OPTS_FILE without collapsing internal whitespace.

set_backend_docker_opts (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: add_backend, update_backend, remove_backend.
  • Behavior: stores or clears docker opts for a backend and strips CR/LF before persistence so state remains single-line.

_parse_docker_opts_to_lines (lib/backends/_parse_docker_opts_to_lines.sh)

  • Entry points: backend create/update and nginx docker-opts validation/runtime assembly.
  • Behavior: parses a shell-like string of docker args into tokens (handles quotes/escapes), including docs-style line continuations (\ + newline/CRLF), and preserves inner quotes for compact JSON env assignments such as KEY=["a"] or KEY={"a":"b"}.

_docker_opts_normalize_profile (lib/backends/validate_docker_opts.sh)

  • Entry points: normalize_docker_opts_for_storage.
  • Behavior: normalizes the docker-opts guardrail profile to backend or nginx (defaults to backend).

_docker_opts_conflicting_option_key (lib/backends/validate_docker_opts.sh)

  • Entry points: normalize_docker_opts_for_storage.
  • Behavior: maps tokens (including short options and --opt=value forms) to blocked option keys for the active profile.

_docker_opts_conflict_owner_guidance (lib/backends/validate_docker_opts.sh)

  • Entry points: normalize_docker_opts_for_storage.
  • Behavior: returns owner-guidance text for rejected docker flags so errors point to the Dockistrate command that controls that concern.

normalize_docker_opts_for_storage (lib/backends/validate_docker_opts.sh)

  • Entry points: backend add/update, start_nginx, set_nginx_docker_opts, and nginx runtime preflight in recreate_nginx_container.
  • Inputs: raw docker opts string, context label, optional profile (backend|nginx).
  • Behavior: tokenizes docker opts, rejects control characters, applies profile-based guardrails, rejects positional image/command tokens plus -- end-of-options marker that can override Dockistrate-managed runtime assembly, rejects options that are missing required values, rejects nginx-profile labels under com.dockistrate.*, preserves quotes in compact JSON env assignments, and returns canonical single-line shell-quoted storage.
  • Guardrails:
    • backend: rejects --name, --network/--net, --rm.
    • nginx: rejects --name, --network/--net, -p/--publish, -P/--publish-all, -v/--volume, --mount, --entrypoint, --rm.

resolve_backend_domain (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: most backend/port commands.
  • Behavior: resolves aliases to primary domains and can log info.

refresh_backend_ips (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx update.
  • Behavior: updates stored IPs in BACKEND_PORTS_FILE if Docker IPs changed; no-op when SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups when changes occur.

refresh_backend_networks (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx update.
  • Behavior: ensures stored network matches actual container network and updates IP accordingly; no-op when SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups when changes occur.

summarize_container_image (lib/backends/common.sh)

  • Entry points: status displays.
  • Behavior: prints a concise image reference for a running container.

Module: ports (lib/ports/*.sh)

add_port_mapping (lib/ports/add_port_mapping.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-port.
  • Inputs: domain, nginx_port, container_port, protocol (http|https|tcp|udp), cert_path, ws, optional http3/alt-svc flags.
  • Behavior: validates inputs, ensures backend exists, skips duplicates before certificate creation, preflights host-port availability for newly introduced listeners, handles HTTPS certificate creation/validation under state/certs, enforces transport-aware listener uniqueness, appends port record.
  • Side effects: writes BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, creates a certificate if needed, backups, update_nginx_config.

update_port_mapping (lib/ports/update_port_mapping.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI update-port.
  • Behavior: selects existing mapping (interactive choice if multiple), checks for duplicate target port before certificate creation, preflights host-port availability when moving a listener to a new host port, updates port/protocol/cert/ws/http3/alt-svc with certificate directories constrained to state/certs, updates path overrides if port changes, clears redirects when non-HTTP.
  • Side effects: edits BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, may create a certificate (suppresses nested nginx updates), backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_port_mapping (lib/ports/remove_port_mapping.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-port.
  • Behavior: removes port mapping and related path overrides; removes TLS overrides if needed.
  • Side effects: edits BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, TLS override files, backups, update_nginx_config.

list_port_mappings (lib/ports/list_port_mappings.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-port-mappings, status, status-all.
  • Behavior: prints human-readable port mappings including transport (http|https|tcp|udp) and HTTPS HTTP/3 state.

set_port_redirect (lib/ports/set_port_redirect.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-port-redirect.
  • Inputs: domain, port, on|off, status code (with optional target port).
  • Behavior: validates the HTTP port mapping, stores redirect flag and code.
  • Side effects: edits BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_port_redirect (lib/ports/remove_port_redirect.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-port-redirect.
  • Behavior: clears redirect flag and code.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

set_port_http3 (lib/ports/port_http3.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-port-http3.
  • Inputs: HTTPS listen port, on|off, optional Alt-Svc mode/value.
  • Behavior: rewrites HTTPS port rows to update http3 and alt_svc state, then regenerates nginx config.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

list_port_http3 (lib/ports/port_http3.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-port-http3.
  • Behavior: lists deduplicated HTTPS port HTTP/3 and Alt-Svc state.

enable_ws (lib/ports/enable_ws.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI enable-ws.
  • Behavior: delegates to _set_ws_flag with yes.

disable_ws (lib/ports/disable_ws.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI disable-ws.
  • Behavior: delegates to _set_ws_flag with no.

_set_ws_flag (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: WS enable/disable.
  • Behavior: updates ws flag in port record; validates port.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

get_backend_ws_flag (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx config generation.
  • Behavior: returns ws flag for a domain and port from the port record.

add_path_option (lib/ports/add_path_option.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-path-option.
  • Inputs: domain, nginx_port, path_prefix, optional ws/redirect/headers plus match/priority/target/rewrite/reason/loc.
  • Behavior: validates path, ensures a compatible port mapping exists, parses path routing options and metadata, stores path record.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

update_path_option (lib/ports/update_path_option.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI update-path-option.
  • Behavior: updates path override attributes (including advanced routing fields), validates, prevents duplicates.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_path_option (lib/ports/remove_path_option.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-path-option.
  • Behavior: deletes a specific path override record.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_all_path_options (lib/ports/remove_all_path_options.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-all-path-options.
  • Behavior: removes all path overrides (optionally for a single domain) and prunes path header includes.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PORTS_FILE and path header includes, backups, update_nginx_config.

list_path_options (lib/ports/list_path_options.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-path-options, status-all.
  • Behavior: lists path overrides with ws/redirect/header settings and advanced routing metadata.

_tcp_listen_in_use (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: add_backend and port mapping operations.
  • Behavior: checks for an existing TCP port mapping on a given port.

_require_port_mapping_for_path (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path option handlers.
  • Behavior: ensures port mapping exists and is not TCP.

_parse_path_ws (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path option handlers.
  • Behavior: validates yes|no|inherit and sets PATH_WS_FLAG.

_parse_path_redirect (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path option handlers.
  • Behavior: validates redirect settings and sets PATH_REDIRECT_FLAG/PATH_REDIRECT_CODE.

_sanitize_path_for_id (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: backups for path overrides.
  • Behavior: sanitizes path into an ID-safe string.

is_valid_path_prefix (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path option validation.
  • Behavior: validates path prefix syntax and rejects Nginx-control characters (#, ;, {, }, control chars).

is_valid_header_set_name (lib/ports/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path header set validation.
  • Behavior: validates header set names for path includes.

Module: nginx (lib/nginx/*.sh)

start_nginx (lib/nginx/start_nginx.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI start-nginx.
  • Inputs: optional --nginx-image <image[:tag]> and optional --docker-opts <opts> ('' clears saved opts).
  • Behavior: validates optional image override, validates/normalizes optional nginx docker opts with nginx-profile guardrails (including rejection of reserved com.dockistrate.* labels), persists changed values to global_settings.csv, rebuilds nginx config, and recreates nginx when image or saved nginx docker opts change on an existing container.
  • Side effects: writes GLOBAL_SETTINGS_FILE, writes nginx config, may recreate container.

recreate_nginx_container (lib/nginx/recreate_nginx_container.sh)

  • Entry points: start_nginx, update_nginx_config, image changes.
  • Behavior: normalizes image ref, validates/parses persisted NGINX_DOCKER_OPTS with nginx-profile guardrails, rejects reserved Dockistrate labels from saved state, computes mapped ports, preflights newly required host ports before container replacement, ensures default network and certificate/webroot directories, builds and mounts the TLS keylog helper before container replacement when decrypt mode is enabled, removes existing container, runs nginx with user-specified docker opts plus Dockistrate-managed labels/mounts/ports/network/image args, connects to backend networks, and reapplies TLS keylog file/dir permissions when decrypt mode is enabled.
  • Side effects: docker rm -f -v, docker run, network connects, may docker pull, creates directories.

reload_nginx_if_running (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: server tokens, config updates.
  • Behavior: runs nginx -s reload in the container if running and returns non-zero on reload failure.

create_nginx_config (lib/nginx/create_nginx_config.sh)

  • Entry points: startup, log field changes.
  • Behavior: runs fix_default_config and cleanup_leftovers, ensures stream dir exists, touches security_rules.inc, creates nginx.conf with log_format from access log fields.
  • Side effects: writes nginx.conf, creates include dirs and placeholder files.

update_nginx_config (lib/nginx/update_nginx_config.sh)

  • Entry points: most configuration changes.
  • Behavior: no-ops when SKIP_UPDATE_NGINX_CONFIG=true; fail-closes on invalid/tampered backend_ports.csv rows (backend/port/path row validation with line-numbered errors), regenerates nginx configs, builds security includes, updates backend IPs/networks (unless SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true), writes HTTP and stream server blocks, recreates nginx container if port set changes, reloads nginx only when the container is reused.
  • Side effects: writes config files, may recreate nginx container or reload nginx, may rollback on failure; skips container changes when SKIP_DOCKER_CHECKS=true.

fix_default_config (lib/nginx/fix_default_config.sh)

  • Entry points: startup and update flows.
  • Behavior: ensures a default self-signed certificate and default server blocks for 80/443 and other mapped ports.
  • Side effects: writes default.conf, generates default certificate files.

normalize_nginx_image (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: image setters, start_nginx.
  • Behavior: appends :latest if no tag/digest provided.

get_all_mapped_ports (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx container creation.
  • Behavior: returns unique mapped ports from BACKEND_PORTS_FILE.

add_nginx_networks (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: after container creation or config update.
  • Behavior: connects nginx container to all backend networks.

remove_unused_nginx_networks (lib/nginx/remove_unused_nginx_networks.sh)

  • Entry points: after config update.
  • Behavior: disconnects nginx from networks no longer used.

stop_nginx (lib/nginx/stop_nginx.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI stop-nginx.
  • Behavior: stops nginx container if running.

remove_nginx (lib/nginx/remove_nginx.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-nginx.
  • Behavior: removes nginx container and attached anonymous volumes.

status (lib/nginx/status.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI status.
  • Behavior: prints the default operator dashboard: proxy status, global settings (including packet capture, TLS-decrypt state, and backup posture), headers, TLS overrides, nginx directive overrides (including strict mode), backend summary with runtime state, ACLs, and container metrics; warns when configured mapped ports differ from runtime published ports; detailed container table lists all Docker containers on the host. In interactive mode, R refreshes the view and Enter returns to the picker.
  • Side effects: reads Docker stats and config files.

status_all (lib/nginx/status.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI status-all.
  • Behavior: extends status with server tokens, access log fields, backend Docker opts, path options, and certificate inventory. In interactive mode, R refreshes the view and Enter returns to the picker.
  • Side effects: reads Docker stats and config files.

get_stats (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: status, status-all.
  • Behavior: returns docker stats info in a pipe-delimited format.

get_sizes (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: status, status-all.
  • Behavior: returns container size information.

tail_proxy_logs (lib/nginx/tail_proxy_logs.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI tail-proxy-logs.
  • Behavior: docker logs -f tailing from nginx container.

describe_image_with_local_state (lib/nginx/describe_image_with_local_state.sh)

  • Entry points: status.
  • Behavior: describes configured image, pull policy, and local availability.

_emit_path_locations (lib/nginx/_emit_path_locations.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: emits location blocks for path overrides, computes effective ws and redirect settings.

_write_location_block (lib/nginx/_write_location_block.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: writes a location block with proxy settings, headers, and optional redirect.

_backend_mtls_directives (lib/nginx/_backend_mtls_directives.sh)

  • Entry points: HTTPS server blocks.
  • Behavior: emits mTLS directives if backend mTLS is enabled.

_ensure_path_header_include (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: path header handling.
  • Behavior: ensures header include file exists for a header set.

_real_ip_directives (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: emits real_ip_* directives based on trusted proxy ranges.

_client_ip_value_var (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: returns $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for for X-Forwarded-For (case-insensitive); otherwise returns $remote_addr so non-XFF client IP headers carry a trusted single client address.

_backend_header_identity_directives (lib/nginx/common.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: emits per-server directives that bind backend header maps to the matched backend context, including alias- and dedicated-host-specific identities.

Module: certs (lib/certs/*.sh)

add_cert (lib/certs/add_cert.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-cert; internal HTTPS flows.
  • Inputs: domain, port_suffix, certificate type, optional paths.
  • Behavior: creates self-signed, Let's Encrypt, or uploaded certificates; ensures permissions; optionally auto-adds an HTTPS port mapping and HTTP redirect; skips the final nginx update when SKIP_UPDATE_NGINX_CONFIG=true (caller-controlled).
  • Side effects: writes certificate files under CERTS_DIR, modifies BACKEND_PORTS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config (unless skipped).
  • Interactive notes: prompts for certificate type and upload paths.

replace_cert (lib/certs/replace_cert.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI replace-cert.
  • Behavior: calls add_cert to create a new certificate, then removes old provider dirs for the same domain/port using safe-delete guards rooted under CERTS_DIR.
  • Side effects: deletes old certificate directories, backups when cleanup occurs (nginx update handled by add_cert).

list_certs (lib/certs/list_certs.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-certs, status-all.
  • Behavior: lists certificate directories, types, ports, and usage based on nginx config and port mappings.

remove_cert (lib/certs/remove_cert.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-cert.
  • Behavior: removes certificate directories only if no port mappings depend on them; certificate/archive/renewal file deletions are validated by safe-delete guards; updates nginx when removal succeeds.
  • Side effects: deletes certificate directories, backups, update_nginx_config.

renew_certs (lib/certs/renew_certs.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI renew-certs.
  • Behavior: scans HTTPS port mappings, checks expiration, renews Let's Encrypt certificates in window, refreshes nginx config.

_renew_letsencrypt_cert (lib/certs/_renew_letsencrypt_cert.sh)

  • Entry points: renew_certs.
  • Behavior: runs certbot in webroot or standalone mode; pulls certbot image per policy; refreshes nginx config before webroot renewal.
  • Side effects: runs Docker certbot, writes certificates under CERTS_DIR, may update_nginx_config.

_cert_expiring_soon (lib/certs/_cert_expiring_soon.sh)

  • Entry points: renew_certs.
  • Behavior: checks if a certificate expires within a window.

_notify_cert_warning (lib/certs/_notify_cert_warning.sh)

  • Entry points: renew_certs.
  • Behavior: logs warnings and optionally emails via mail or sendmail.

check_certbot_installed (lib/certs/check_certbot_installed.sh)

  • Entry points: certificate creation/renewal.
  • Behavior: no-op when using Docker-based certbot.

cert_created_timestamp (lib/certs/cert_created_timestamp.sh)

  • Entry points: list_certs.
  • Behavior: returns file mtime in a cross-platform way.

Module: TLS (lib/tls/*.sh)

set_tls_protocols (lib/tls/set_tls_protocols.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-tls-protocols.
  • Behavior: validates protocol list, stores global setting, updates nginx.

set_tls_ciphers (lib/tls/set_tls_ciphers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-tls-ciphers.
  • Behavior: validates cipher string using openssl ciphers, stores global setting, updates nginx.

set_port_tls_protocols (lib/tls/set_port_tls_protocols.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-port-tls-protocols.
  • Behavior: validates the HTTPS port mapping, stores per-port override, updates nginx.

remove_port_tls_protocols (lib/tls/remove_port_tls_protocols.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-port-tls-protocols.
  • Behavior: removes per-port protocol overrides and updates nginx.

set_port_tls_ciphers (lib/tls/set_port_tls_ciphers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-port-tls-ciphers.
  • Behavior: validates the HTTPS port mapping and cipher string, stores override, updates nginx.

remove_port_tls_ciphers (lib/tls/remove_port_tls_ciphers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-port-tls-ciphers.
  • Behavior: removes per-port cipher overrides and updates nginx.

get_port_tls_protocols (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation and prompts.
  • Behavior: returns per-port override or global default.

get_port_tls_ciphers (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation and prompts.
  • Behavior: returns per-port override or global default.

_validate_tls_protocols (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: TLS setters.
  • Behavior: validates TLS protocol tokens.

_validate_tls_ciphers (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: TLS setters.
  • Behavior: validates cipher string via openssl ciphers.

validate_tls_settings_for_render (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: update_nginx_config, fix_default_config.
  • Behavior: fail-closed validation for persisted global and per-port TLS protocol/cipher state before config rendering.

_require_https_port_mapping (lib/tls/common.sh)

  • Entry points: per-port TLS setters.
  • Behavior: ensures a port is mapped and uses HTTPS.

Module: HTTP Version (lib/http_version/*.sh)

set_http_version (lib/http_version/set_http_version.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-http-version.
  • Behavior: sets global HTTP version, saves config, updates nginx.

set_backend_http_version (lib/http_version/set_backend_http_version.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-backend-http-version.
  • Behavior: stores per-backend HTTP version override, updates nginx.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_HTTP_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_backend_http_version (lib/http_version/remove_backend_http_version.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-backend-http-version.
  • Behavior: removes override for a backend.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_HTTP_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

get_backend_http_version (lib/http_version/get_backend_http_version.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation and status.
  • Behavior: returns per-backend override or global.

get_proxy_http_version (lib/http_version/get_proxy_http_version.sh)

  • Entry points: config generation.
  • Behavior: maps http2 to 1.1 for upstream proxy_http_version.

Module: headers (lib/headers/*.sh)

set_header (lib/headers/set_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-header and update-header.
  • Behavior: validates header name, type, and value (rejects control characters), stores global header entry.
  • Side effects: updates CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_header (lib/headers/remove_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-header.
  • Behavior: removes a global header entry.
  • Side effects: updates CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

list_headers (lib/headers/list_headers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-headers.
  • Behavior: prints global headers.

set_backend_header (lib/headers/set_backend_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI add-backend-header and update-backend-header.
  • Behavior: validates backend, header, and value (rejects control characters), stores per-backend header override.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_backend_header (lib/headers/remove_backend_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-backend-header.
  • Behavior: removes a per-backend header override.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

list_backend_headers (lib/headers/list_backend_headers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI list-backend-headers.
  • Behavior: lists backend headers (all or for a domain).

remove_all_headers (lib/headers/remove_all_headers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-all-headers.
  • Behavior: clears global header list.
  • Side effects: updates CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_all_backend_headers (lib/headers/remove_all_backend_headers.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-all-backend-headers.
  • Behavior: clears backend header overrides (all or for a domain).
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_HEADERS_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

get_global_header_value (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive defaults.
  • Behavior: returns a header value from CUSTOM_HEADERS_FILE.

get_backend_header_value (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: interactive defaults.
  • Behavior: returns a backend header override value.

set_hsts (lib/headers/set_hsts.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-hsts.
  • Behavior: sets or removes global HSTS header.

set_backend_hsts (lib/headers/set_backend_hsts.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-backend-hsts.
  • Behavior: sets or removes backend-specific HSTS header.

set_csp (lib/headers/set_csp.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-csp.
  • Behavior: sets or removes global CSP header.

set_backend_csp (lib/headers/set_backend_csp.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-backend-csp.
  • Behavior: sets or removes backend-specific CSP header.

set_client_ip_header (lib/headers/set_client_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-client-ip-header.
  • Behavior: sets global client IP header (or disables), saves config, updates nginx; X-Forwarded-For uses chain semantics and other header names forward the trusted client address via $remote_addr.

set_proxy_ip_header (lib/headers/set_proxy_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-proxy-ip-header.
  • Behavior: sets global proxy IP header (or disables), saves config, updates nginx.

set_backend_client_ip_header (lib/headers/set_backend_client_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-backend-client-ip-header.
  • Behavior: sets per-backend client IP header override or disables.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_CLIENT_IP_HEADER_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_backend_client_ip_header (lib/headers/remove_backend_client_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-backend-client-ip-header.
  • Behavior: removes per-backend client IP header override.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_CLIENT_IP_HEADER_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

set_backend_proxy_ip_header (lib/headers/set_backend_proxy_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI set-backend-proxy-ip-header.
  • Behavior: sets per-backend proxy IP header override or disables.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PROXY_IP_HEADER_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

remove_backend_proxy_ip_header (lib/headers/remove_backend_proxy_ip_header.sh)

  • Entry points: CLI remove-backend-proxy-ip-header.
  • Behavior: removes per-backend proxy IP header override.
  • Side effects: updates BACKEND_PROXY_IP_HEADER_FILE, backups, update_nginx_config.

get_backend_client_ip_header (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx config generation.
  • Behavior: returns per-backend client IP header override or global default.

get_backend_proxy_ip_header (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: nginx config generation.
  • Behavior: returns per-backend proxy IP header override or global default.

_build_header_files (lib/headers/_build_header_files.sh)

  • Entry points: update_nginx_config.
  • Behavior: validates persisted header type/domain/name/value rows, then generates Nginx include files for global and backend headers plus backend header maps keyed by the server-local backend identity variable.

_escape_header_value (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: header file generation.
  • Behavior: escapes backslashes and quotes for Nginx config values.

_validate_persisted_header_row (lib/headers/common.sh)

  • Entry points: _build_header_files.
  • Behavior: fail-closed validation for persisted global/backend header rows before config rendering.
